title: acmella pusilla
intro: Acmella pusilla, also known as Suckers Plant or Toothache Plant, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate toothache pain, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. Acmella pusilla is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, where it can be consumed or applied to the affected area to reap its health benefits.
